title: Critical Bug: Google Auth Initialization Runtime Deadlock Under Telephony Footprint MCC 421
author: executionerhosamaldubai2030-a11y
body: ### Environment & Context - **Target Network Footprint:** MCC 421 (Yemen Carrier Infrastructure) - **Subsystem:** Google Play Services / Auth Identity Pipeline - **Core Platform State:** Trapped under May Security Patch Level (Google Play System updates fail consecutively 3-5 times over the last 14 days). ### Description of the Issue A critical framework-level runtime deadlock occurs during any core authentication or synchronization attempt when the device is registered under the Yemeni telephony footprint (MCC 421). Instead of gracefully falling back or throwing an unhandled exception, the identity wizard goes into a perpetual loading sequence, freezing the UI completely. This issue behavior has been verified as a systemic carrier/regional network block rather than a localized device malfunction. ### Diagnostic Evidence Mapped 1. **Replication Video showing the runtime deadlock:** https://drive.google.com/file/d/10-4AcDwm4EvmVP1QhE9MS-zt70GK2Khm/view?usp=drivesdk 2. **System Bug Report Logs (Telephony & Auth traces):** https://drive.google.com/file/d/1yq-7vYrjthAneJlhWnVmEZ9-klymVJ7T/view?usp=drivesdk *Note: The Android Security Team reviewed this and confirmed it as a functional platform framework/connectivity bug that needs immediate routing to the identity and connectivity infrastructure maintainers.* Please investigate the regional network handler constraints causing this total ecosystem lockout for users on this network footprint.

title: [Security] Exported Activity (TodoActivity) without permission protection
author: airdropp20208-star
body:
## Summary
Static analysis found 1 exported activity without permission protection.

**File:** app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml:29
**Activity:** TodoActivity with 

External apps can launch this activity with crafted intents.
**Fix:** Add  or validate intent extras in onCreate().

**Scanner:** semgrep 1.162.0 on proot-distro Ubuntu 25.10

title: Using Mirrors to Transform Light and Space in Interior Design
author: bowerbirdlab
body:
In[ interior designers in Goa](https://bowerbirdlab.com/portfolio/keshaa-salon-spa/), mirrors are far more than decorative objects. When used thoughtfully, they become powerful design tools that can influence how light travels, how space is perceived, and how a room feels.
A well-placed mirror can brighten a dim corner, visually expand a compact room, or highlight a beautiful view. Designers often use mirrors strategically to enhance natural light, improve spatial balance, and introduce depth within interiors.
Understanding where and how to place mirrors can dramatically change the atmosphere of a home without altering the [architects in Goa](https://bowerbirdlab.com/portfolio/crest-grande-apartments-socorro-goa/) itself.

Reflecting Light to Brighten Interiors
One of the most effective uses of mirrors is to enhance natural light within a room. By positioning mirrors opposite or adjacent to windows, daylight can be reflected deeper into the space, reducing shadows and creating a brighter environment.
This technique is particularly useful in homes where certain areas receive limited natural light. A well-positioned mirror can distribute light evenly across the room, making interiors feel open and vibrant throughout the day.
Designers also use mirrors to reflect outdoor views such as gardens, courtyards, or landscapes. This visual extension subtly brings the outside environment into the interior space, creating a stronger connection with nature.

Enhancing the Sense of Space
Mirrors are widely used to visually expand compact spaces. In smaller rooms, large mirrors can create the illusion of additional depth, making the room appear larger than it actually is.
For narrow rooms or corridors, placing a long mirror along one wall can balance the proportions of the space and reduce the feeling of confinement. Similarly, tall mirrors can emphasize verticality and make ceilings feel higher.
These simple interventions allow designers to modify spatial perception without structural changes.

Amplifying Natural and Artificial Light
Large mirrors, especially frameless or minimal designs, are particularly effective at capturing and redistributing natural light. They act almost like secondary light sources within the room.
In spaces with limited daylight, mirrors can also enhance artificial lighting. When placed near lamps or wall lights, they help diffuse light more evenly across the room, creating a softer and more welcoming atmosphere.
Full-length mirrors are often used in living rooms, bedrooms, or hallways for this reason—they combine functionality with spatial enhancement.

Choosing Mirrors That Complement Interior Style
Beyond their functional benefits, mirrors also contribute to the visual identity of a space. The style, shape, and frame of a mirror should align with the overall design language of the room.
Minimal or frameless mirrors work well in modern and contemporary interiors, reinforcing clean lines and simplicity. Ornate framed mirrors with detailed craftsmanship complement more classical or traditional spaces, adding richness and elegance.
In eclectic or bohemian interiors, mixing mirrors of different shapes and sizes can create a layered visual composition that adds personality to the room.
Selecting mirrors carefully ensures that they enhance both the aesthetics and spatial quality of the interior.

Practical Considerations for Mirror Placement
While mirrors can dramatically improve interiors, thoughtful placement is essential to avoid visual clutter. Mirrors should ideally reflect appealing views, [architectural features](https://medium.com/@bowerbirdlab/apartment-interior-design-in-goa-where-lifestyle-meets-architecture-03b1f806f441), or open space rather than busy or cluttered areas.
Keeping mirrors clean and free from excessive reflections helps maintain their ability to brighten and expand a room.
In many Indian homes, mirror placement is also influenced by traditional guidelines such as Vastu Shastra. Mirrors are commonly recommended on north or east-facing walls, while reflections of beds or main doors are generally avoided.

A Simple Tool with Powerful Impact
Mirrors offer one of the simplest ways to transform an interior. With careful placement and thoughtful design, they can introduce light, enhance spatial perception, and add visual interest to a room.
In architecture and interior design, small design decisions often have the greatest impact. Mirrors are a perfect example of how a simple element can significantly influence how a space is experienced.At Bowerbird Lab, we believe that thoughtful design lies in understanding how materials, light, and spatial perception interact. Even a subtle design element like a mirror can reshape the character of a room when used with intention.

Want to Create Beautifully Designed Interiors?
Thoughtful interior design is about more than furniture and finishes — it’s about understanding how light, materials, and spatial perception work together. Elements like mirrors, when used intentionally, can transform the way a space feels and functions.
At[ Bowerbird Lab](https://bowerbirdlab.com/), we design interiors that balance aesthetics, light, and functionality to create spaces that feel both elegant and comfortable.
If you’re planning a new home or interior renovation, our team would be happy to help bring your vision to life.
[Contact Bowerbird Lab to start planning your space today.](https://bowerbirdlab.com/contact-us/)

title: Apartment Interior Design in Goa: Where Lifestyle Meets Architecture
author: bowerbirdlab
body:
Goa apartments are no longer just holiday homes — they are becoming permanent residences, second homes, and lifestyle investments. Interior design plays a crucial role in shaping how these spaces feel, perform, and age over time.

A thoughtfully designed apartment balances coastal comfort with urban sophistication, responding to humidity, sunlight, compact layouts, and the relaxed rhythm of Goan living.

Great interiors don’t just look good — they make everyday life easier, calmer, and more refined.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Design for Natural Flow
[Apartments in Goa](https://bowerbirdlab.com/) thrive when they feel open, bright, and breathable. Maximizing daylight, maintaining cross-ventilation, and keeping circulation zones clear are fundamental starting points.

Neutral backdrops, light finishes, and uncluttered layouts enhance the sense of space — especially in 1BHK and 2BHK units. Sliding doors, soft partitions, and balcony integrations blur indoor–outdoor boundaries and reflect the coastal lifestyle.

A single visual anchor — a feature wall, sculptural light, or framed sea-facing view — often becomes the soul of the apartment.

Coastal Influence, Contemporary Language
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Rather than literal beach themes, successful interiors borrow subtly from Goa’s natural palette and heritage.

Warm whites, sand tones, soft greys, sage greens, and muted terracottas pair beautifully with cane, teak, flatted shutters, lime plaster finishes, and textured fabrics. These elements create homes that feel grounded, modern, and timeless — perfect for both permanent residents and weekend retreats.

Materials That Age Gracefully
The coastal climate demands smart material choices.

Moisture-resistant plywood, marine-grade laminates, quartz countertops, vitrified or engineered flooring, and anti-rust hardware ensure longevity. Matte finishes hide wear better than gloss, while washable paints and sealed stone surfaces simplify upkeep.

Furniture should prioritize comfort and proportion — never overcrowding compact rooms, yet generous enough for daily living.

Lighting, Storage & Liveability
Layered lighting defines the mood of a Goan apartment.

Ambient ceiling lights, warm bedside lamps, cove lighting in living areas, and soft balcony illumination create depth and warmth after sunset. Dimmers allow residents to shift from work mode to evening calm effortlessly.

Smart storage — floor-to-ceiling wardrobes, concealed utility cabinets, floating TV units, and integrated shoe storage — keeps homes organized without visual heaviness.

Become a Medium member
Low-maintenance surfaces and easy-to-clean layouts protect the interiors over time.

Budget Reality: Turnkey Apartment Interiors
For a well-executed 1BHK or[ 2BHK apartment in Goa](https://bowerbirdlab.com/apartment-interior-design-in-goa-where-lifestyle-meets-architecture/), a turnkey interior budget generally falls between ₹18–30 lakh, depending on finishes, carpentry complexity, appliances, and custom detailing.

This usually includes:

Complete design & execution
Modular kitchen with appliances
Wardrobes & storage systems
Living and bedroom furniture
Lighting, bathrooms & soft furnishings
Balcony enhancements
The focus is on creating a cohesive lifestyle environment — not decorative excess.

Do Good Interiors Add Long-Term Value?
Absolutely.

Apartments with professionally designed interiors:

Command higher resale value
Rent faster and at better rates
Age more gracefully
Deliver stronger everyday comfort
Two apartments in the same building can feel worlds apart. The difference is rarely the square footage — it’s the planning, detailing, and material intelligence.

Good design quietly elevates daily life.

“In residential interiors, design is not an upgrade.
It’s the foundation of how you live.”

When architecture, climate response, and emotion align, [apartment interiors in Goa](https://bowerbirdlab.com/) become more than beautiful spaces — they become homes that feel effortless, timeless, and deeply connected to their surroundings.

title: ## Code Review
author: Boupolin9477
body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a new file named 'Coffee Area' which appears to be empty and unrelated to the project's purpose. This seems to be an accidental addition. My review recommends removing this file to maintain the project's integrity and cleanliness.

title: Creating or editing a task shows the snackbar more than once when navigating
author: KahiloMassango
body: After creating or editing a task, the snackbar displays the message according to the action, but when navigating to a different screen and then returning to tasks screen, the message is shown again.

title: ## Code Review
author: Mdrieaz
body:
## Code Review
This pull request effectively introduces support for HEIC UltraHDR image capture. The approach of extending the existing `Camera2UltraHDRCapture` class is sound, and the necessary API changes (like using `CaptureRequest.JPEG_ORIENTATION` instead of EXIF manipulation for HEIC) are correctly implemented. The SDK version updates and new sample demo registration are also appropriate.

Overall, the changes are well-done and enhance the camera sample capabilities. I have one suggestion for improving the robustness of the file creation logic for future extensions.

### Summary of Findings
* **File Extension Handling in `createFile`**: The `createFile` method determines file extensions based on the image format. The current `else` case defaults to `.heic`, which is fine for this PR but could be problematic if other non-HEIC UltraHDR formats are supported in the future via subclassing. A more explicit handling of known formats and a clearer strategy for unknown ones would improve robustness. (Commented with medium severity)
* **Copyright Year**: The new file `samples/camera/camera2/src/main/java/com/example/platform/camera/imagecapture/Camera2HeicUltraHDRCapture.kt` has a copyright year of 2025. Typically, this is the year of creation (e.g., 2024) or matches the project's existing files (e.g., `Camera2UltraHDRCapture.kt` uses 2023). Please verify if 2025 is intentional or a typo. (Not commented due to review settings: low severity)
* **KDoc Documentation**: Consider adding KDoc comments to the new class `Camera2HeicUltraHDRCapture` and its overridden `ULTRAHDR_FORMAT` property. Also, the newly `open` base class `Camera2UltraHDRCapture` and its `ULTRAHDR_FORMAT` property could benefit from KDocs explaining their roles and extensibility. This would improve code clarity and maintainability. (Not commented due to review settings: low severity)

### Merge Readiness
The pull request is in good shape and introduces a valuable feature. I recommend addressing the medium-severity comment regarding file extension handling in `createFile` to enhance future maintainability. Once that's considered, the PR should be ready for merging. As an AI reviewer, I am not authorized to approve pull requests; please ensure further review and approval from authorized team members.

title: Clicking the return button of AddEditTaskTopAppBar causes a blank interface.
author: yaosming
body: When the return button of AddEditTaskTopAppBar is clicked and then the openDrawer button of TasksTopAppBar is immediately clicked within a short period of time, a blank interface will occur.

title: task hijacking bug
author: yssssyy
body:
Task stack hijacking affects apps with startup modes of singletask and standard

We hijacked the task stack of the application by setting the same taskAffinity. When the user clicks on the application, what is actually displayed is the activity of the attack application we wrote (there is no interface switching process in the entire process, which is very hidden and difficult for the user to discover). By designing the activity in this way, we can mimic the login interface of the application, induce the user to log in, and steal the user's private password information

Reproduction method:
Write an attack application that hijacks the application's task stack by setting the taskAffinity attribute to the package name of soul
Expected behaviour
Repair plan

Set the taskAffinity property of the application's activity to empty

When the APP is initially enabled, check whether the number of front-end tasks in the activity stack is greater than the initial set value. If it is greater than the initial set value, it is necessary to draw the user's attention to the possibility of disguised malicious attacks in the future. Do not output sensitive information on the page

Example code:

val activityManager = getSystemService(ACTIVITY_SERVICE) as ActivityManager

val appTasks: List = activityManager.getAppTasks()

if (appTasks.isNotEmpty()) {

Val initial Activity Count=5//Assuming that the initial set number of front-end task activities is 5

val taskInfo: RecentTaskInfo = appTasks[0].taskInfo

val numActivities: Int = taskInfo.numActivities

if (numActivities > initialActivityCount) {

//If the number of activities in the current front-end task is greater than the initial set value, a Toast prompt will pop up to remind the user to pay attention to safety

Toast.makeText (this, "Warning: The current front-end task has an abnormal number of activities, which may pose a security risk. Please be careful not to enter sensitive information on subsequent pages! ", Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show()

}

}

The specific attack video has been attached

[攻击视频1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19809478/1.zip)

title: [BUG]StrandHogg2.0 Restoration suggestions
author: 1pear1
body:
[Vulnerability Title] Domain name and scope affected by the vulnerability, parameters involved, vulnerability type, etc.
Vulnerability Type: Android Task Stack Hijacking
Affected Scope: Applications with standard startup mode

[Vulnerability Description] Contains the URL, parameters, application version, etc. involved in the vulnerability
The attacker deliberately did not set the FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK flag of the disguised page and placed a disguised page in the task stack of the target application.
When the user clicks the target application icon, pressing the back key will start the Activity of the malicious application written by the attacker. It is difficult for the user to distinguish between the normal page and the disguised page. The attacker can use this to imitate the login interface of the target application and induce the user to enter the account and password, thereby stealing the user's private information.

[Reproduction method] Describe the vulnerability reproduction sequence according to logic. If you use a tool to reproduce the vulnerability, you should provide the tool name.
1. Write an attack application and set the target application package name and activity name.
2. Install the attack application on the test device.
3. Start the target application and observe whether the task stack is successfully hijacked and the activity of the attack application is started.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/681ac1c9-a1f0-438d-b5e5-d410d3eecf04
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bdd25dd5-d27e-4b42-a8ea-19c808e22f7e

[Proof of exploitation] Contains the vulnerability impact description and the proof of exploitation, which are generally provided in the form of screenshots.
The attack application is successfully started. When the user initially starts the APP, pressing the back button will display the counterfeit target application interface. The information entered by the user is captured by the attack application. The specific process is shown in the attachment.

[Repair plan] Provide at least one executable repair suggestion, which can provide code-level repair suggestions or protection strategies.
1. Without affecting the normal function of the application, try to set android:launchMode="singleTask". When the application is first started, the disguised page will be cleared in the activity stack, so it will not be attacked in this case;
2. Without affecting the normal function of the application, try to set android:exported="true" to prevent it from being started from an external application;
3. When the application is started, count the number of activities in the foreground task in the task stack. If the number is greater than the initial setting value, prompt the user that there may be a disguised malicious attack page, and recommend that the user not enter sensitive information.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/012bf24b-0358-49fc-afb6-36da03b1cf2c

Sample code:
`val activityManager = getSystemService(ACTIVITY_SERVICE) as ActivityManager
        val appTasks: List<AppTask> = activityManager.getAppTasks()

        if (appTasks.isNotEmpty()) {
            val initialActivityCount = 1
            val taskInfo: RecentTaskInfo = appTasks[0].taskInfo
            val numActivities: Int = taskInfo.numActivities

            if (numActivities > initialActivityCount) {
                Toast.makeText(this, "Warning: The number of activities in the current foreground task is abnormal, which may pose a security risk. Please be careful not to enter sensitive information on subsequent pages!", Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show()
            }
        }`

title: Using edgeToEdge breaks windowSoftInputMode
author: Jean-Daniel
body:
When using edgeToEdge, the view size is no longer adjusting when the keyboard is visible, resulting in buttons hidden behind the keyboard.


Without edgeToEdge call in the main Activity:

With EdgeToEdge call in the main Activity.

title: how to make an indent from the status bar and the screen cutout?
author: iFr0z
body:
![xFyxbd5i](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/676b69b0-f224-4342-bc02-4c1161544223)

```
inline fun View.snackBarTop(string: String, length: Int = LENGTH_LONG, f: Snackbar.() -> Unit) {
    val snackBarTop = make(this, string, length)
    val snackBarView = snackBarTop.view
    val params: LayoutParams = snackBarView.layoutParams as LayoutParams
    params.gravity = Gravity.TOP
    ViewCompat.setOnApplyWindowInsetsListener(snackBarView) { _, windowInsets ->
        val insets = windowInsets.getInsets(
            WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars() or WindowInsetsCompat.Type.displayCutout()
        )
        snackBarView.translationY = insets.top.toFloat()
        WindowInsetsCompat.CONSUMED
    }
    snackBarView.layoutParams = params
    snackBarTop.f()
    snackBarTop.show()
}
```
